purpose: Deepen graph-to-multiplication connection; reinforce ×10 with explicit context link from M1-M6.
visual: SPLIT DISPLAY: Left side shows a simple Picture Graph with scale of 10 (3-4 categories, one row with 3 symbols highlighted). Right side shows Equation Builder with `3 × 10 = [___]` (factors pre-filled). Methods C/D active.
guide: "Check this out. Remember reading graphs with scales? Here's a graph with a scale of 10. That row has 3 symbols. 3 symbols, each worth 10. That's 3 groups of 10. You've been doing this all along — reading graph scales WAS multiplication. Now find the product of 3 times 10. Place the tile."
student_action: Equation Builder Methods C or D (product tile only — factors pre-filled)
on_correct: "3 times 10 equals 30. Ends in zero so it matches the multiples of 10s pattern. The graph shows 30 items in that category. Same math you just learned."
remediation_note: Student counts by 10s three times to compute 3 × 10 = 30.
design_note: Graph callback — brief, explicit, connecting M1-M6 graph scales to M9 multiplication. NOT an extended graph-reading activity. Uses ×10 to reinforce the newest factor family (addresses audit Issue #7: ×10 coverage gap). Guide narrates the connection; student's only task is computing the product.
